methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Quantum Brilliance is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation framework draws from a broader comp universe of 11 quantum computing companies—3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including Q-CTRL, IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable valuation approach. Using the $50M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement yields an implied valuation of $95M. Peer-median triangulation was not feasible due to insufficient datapoints within narrow peer filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier cohorts yielded fewer than three comparable companies), and a broader cross-sector median would introduce material bias given sector-specific dynamics in quantum computing valuations.

The triangulated valuation range is $81M–$109M, with a central estimate of $95M. This analysis relies on a single robust method; consequently, key uncertainties center on stage-progression multiplier stability within frontier hardware sectors and the degree to which Quantum Brilliance's specific technology differentiation and IP portfolio merit a premium or discount relative to the assumed 1.9x multiplier. Additional comparables at similar stage and valuation tiers would strengthen confidence in this estimate.
